channel.title: Load Simulation on whhong's blog
channel.description: Recent content in Load Simulation on whhong's blog

item.title: TurbSim Wind Field Modeling and OpenFAST Inflow Setup Guide
item.description:
Input Parameters

Random Number Generation Parameters

Parameter Description Recommended Value
RandSeed1 Random seed generator, used with RandSeed2 Set according to requirements
RandSeed2 Random number generator specification Recommended “RanLux”

Output Format Parameters

Parameter Description Options
WrAdFF Output format specification, meets AeroDyn requirements “True” or “False”, outputs .bts file

Time Parameters

Parameter Description Recommended Value
TimeStep Time step, determines maximum frequency 0.05 seconds (corresponds to 20Hz)
Analysis Time Simulation duration At least 600 seconds
Usable Time Usable time Set to “All”

Grid Parameters

Parameter Description Notes
NumGrid_Z Z-direction grid points Integer greater than 1
NumGrid_Y Y-direction grid points Odd number means center point falls on tower centerline
HubHt Hub height (m) Reference height for grid positioning
GridHeight Grid height 1/2 of grid height should be less than HubHt
GridWidth Grid width Ensure blade rotation stays within grid range

IEC Standard Parameters

Parameter Description Options
IECstandard IEC standard type 1: 61400-1, 2: 61400-2, 3: 61400-3 (offshore)
IECturbc Turbulence intensity class A (highest), B (default), C
IEC_WindType IEC turbulence model NTM (Normal Turbulence), ETM (Extreme Turbulence), NWM (Extreme Wind)

OpenFAST Common Errors and Solutions

1. Blade-Tower Collision

Problem Description

Blade collides with tower during simulation.

Possible Causes and Solutions

Cause 1: Improper Initial Pitch Angle Setting

  • Problem: Initial blade pitch angle set unreasonably, causing blades too close to tower
  • Solution:
    • Check BlPitch parameter in .fst file
    • Set reasonable initial pitch angle (typically 3-5 degrees)
    • Ensure all blades have consistent pitch angles

Cause 2: Tower Shadow Effect Parameter Issues

  • Problem: Tower shadow effect calculation causes abnormal aerodynamic forces
  • Solution:
    • Check TwrShadow and TwrPotent settings in AeroDyn
    • Ensure tower geometry parameters are correct

Cause 3: Structural Model Problems

  • Problem: Unreasonable stiffness or mass distribution of blades or tower
  • Solution:
    • Check Blade and Tower property files
    • Verify modal frequencies are reasonable
    • Ensure mass distribution matches reality

2. Numerical Instability

Symptoms

Simulation diverges in short time, showing extremely large displacement or velocity values.

Solutions

Check Time Step

  • Reduce DT time step (try 0.001 or smaller)
  • Ensure CFL condition is satisfied

Check Numerical Damping

  • Adjust damping parameters in CompElast or CompSub
  • Add artificial damping to stabilize calculation

3. Wind Field File Errors

Symptoms

Prompt says wind field file format error or cannot read.

Solutions

Check TurbSim Output

  • Confirm .bts file is generated correctly
  • Check if file path is set correctly
  • Verify WindType setting in InflowWind (should be 2 for .bts file)

Check Wind Field Parameter Matching

  • Confirm TurbSim’s HubHt matches OpenFAST
  • Check if GridWidth and GridHeight satisfy blade sweep range

4. Controller-Related Issues

Symptoms

Control-related errors occur or turbine behavior is abnormal.

Solutions

Check DISCON.dll

  • Ensure controller DLL file path is correct
  • Verify controller parameter file (usually DISCON.IN)
  • Check DLL_FileName and DLL_InFile settings

Check Initial Conditions

  • Ensure initial rotational speed and pitch angle are within reasonable range
  • Check correspondence between generator torque and rotational speed

Debugging Suggestions

Step-by-Step Troubleshooting

  1. Simplify Model: First test with rigid blades and fixed tower
  2. Gradually Add Complexity: Sequentially enable elastic blades, tower, controller
  3. Check Each Component: Separately verify AeroDyn, ElastoDyn, InflowWind settings

Common Checklist

  • All input file paths are correct
  • Geometric parameters are consistent (hub height, blade length, etc.)
  • Time step is appropriate
  • Initial conditions are reasonable
  • Wind field files are correctly generated and readable
  • Controller parameters are set correctly

Reference Materials

  • OpenFAST Official Documentation: https://openfast.readthedocs.io/
  • NREL Forum: https://forums.nrel.gov/
  • GitHub Issues Page: https://github.com/OpenFAST/openfast/issues

This article is written based on OpenFAST v3.5.0 and TurbSim v2.0. Different versions may have parameter differences.
